commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=98e38ad3874e828e941cfab41b5d66efe0809e4d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the CLI JSONL streaming parser to treat Claude CLI assistant partial messages as incremental streaming deltas and adds parser coverage for partials, deduplication, filtering, and mixed content.

PR surface: Source +50, Tests +135. Total +185 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence current-main reproduction was established in this read-only pass. The linked issue gives detailed Windows/WebChat/TUI symptoms, but this PR still needs raw live Claude stream-json showing the claimed top-level assistant partials.
commands.review_followup: The blocker is maintainer/contributor proof of the raw Claude CLI live output and the right fix boundary; automation cannot safely infer or choose a vendor-contract deviation.

target.title: fix: relay Claude CLI assistant partial messages as streaming deltas
target.body:
## Summary

The CLI JSONL streaming parser (`createCliJsonlStreamingParser`) only recognized `stream_event` events with `content_block_delta`/`text_delta` payloads. Claude CLI with `--include-partial-messages` (always enabled for the claude-cli backend) also emits `{"type":"assistant","message":{...}}` events carrying the accumulated text. When the CLI suppresses or omits raw `stream_event` lines in favor of these partial messages, zero streaming deltas reached the gateway, leaving WebChat and TUI surfaces without any progress until the turn closed.

Adds `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` that extracts text from `assistant` partial messages and computes the delta against the previously accumulated text. When both `stream_event` deltas and `assistant` partials arrive for the same text, the partials are naturally deduplicated because the accumulated length has already advanced.

Closes #86050

## Verification

22 scenarios pass across the CLI output parser, including 6 new scenarios covering assistant partial message extraction, incremental delta computation, deduplication with stream_event, non-Claude backend filtering, tool_use-only skipping, and mixed text/tool_use content.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway streams zero Claude CLI assistant deltas to WebChat/TUI during turns; surfaces only render the final assembled message after the turn closes (#86050)

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node 22, patched source from branch fix/gateway-stream-relay

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Replayed real Claude CLI JSONL assistant partial message events through the parser with node to demonstrate incremental delta extraction

Evidence after fix:
```
BEFORE fix: assistant partial messages are ignored, zero deltas relayed
AFTER fix: parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta extracts incremental deltas:
  delta="Hello"  accumulated="Hello"
  delta=" world"  accumulated="Hello world"
  delta=", how are you?"  accumulated="Hello world, how are you?"
Total deltas relayed: 3
Gateway would send 3 chat state:delta WebSocket messages
```

Observed result after fix: The streaming parser correctly extracts incremental deltas from Claude CLI assistant partial messages. Each partial message carries the accumulated text so far; the parser computes the new text by slicing from the previously seen length. The gateway relay code forwards these as chat state:delta WebSocket messages to WebChat/TUI without further changes needed.

What was not tested: Live Claude CLI subprocess with real Anthropic API credentials producing actual partial message JSONL events
target.author: sebtardif
